text:
Water quality is significant for human health, biodiversity, industrial processes and ecosystem integrity. Water quality has declined in recent years because of increasing amounts of pollutants and type of contaminants in water bodies. This paper investigated the development of resilient models for forecasting Water Quality Index (WQI) and Water Quality Classification (WQC) metrics which are critical in determining the validity of water. The model developed in this research was enhanced using a grid search optimisation technique in which the hyperparameters of eight models were optimised. The models developed for forecasting WQC were four classifiers namely Random Forest (RF), Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost), Gradient Boosting (GB) and Adaptive Boosting (AdaBoost) while those developed for forecasting WQI were four regressors namely K-Nearest Neighbor (KNN), Decision Tree (DT), Support Vector Regressor (SVR) and Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP).

Experimental research of computer science and mathematics knowledge in water resources field is rarely seen. Currently, there is no deep research or integrated solution based on the machine learning and optimization theories. In the experiment, the dataset with 1,991 samples and 7 variables were preprocessed through mean replacement and standardization. Then, all samples are divided into training set and test set. And the corresponding evaluation indicators for classification models are the evaluation indicators such as accuracy, recall, precision, Matthew’s correlation coefficient (MCC), F1-score and the corresponding evaluation indicators for regression models are Mean Absolute Error (MAE), Median Absolute Error (MedAE), Mean Square Error (MSE) and the coefficient of determination (R²) are taken. According to the experimental results, the Gradient Boosting model has the highest accuracy of 99.50%. The MLP regressor has the largest R² value of 99.8% among all the regression models.
